highlights.name: Embedded C
highlights.usage: Bare-metal and RTOS firmware on MCUs — drivers, ISRs, and deterministic control loops in automotive, industrial, and appliance ECUs.

highlights.name: C++
highlights.usage: Structured firmware modules and state machines on Cortex-M platforms where encapsulation helps across multi-ECU product lines.

highlights.name: Python
highlights.usage: Build automation, HIL helpers, CAN tooling, and manufacturing scripts that cut flash/test cycle time on the production floor.

highlights.name: Cortex-M4/M7
highlights.usage: Control and connectivity ECUs in refrigeration, off-highway, and industrial machines — from bring-up through field diagnostics.

highlights.name: embOS
highlights.usage: Hard real-time tasking on Cortex-M4 refrigeration controllers, including watchdog and diagnostic services.

highlights.name: MQX
highlights.usage: Cross-generation firmware on Cortex-M7 targets where the same features must ship on an older RTOS stack.

highlights.name: FreeRTOS
highlights.usage: Portable task scheduling for IoT and industrial nodes when a lightweight, widely supported kernel is required.

highlights.name: CAN / J1939
highlights.usage: Vehicle and machine networks — multi-ECU refrigeration, construction, and agriculture platforms exchanging commands and diagnostics.

highlights.name: UART / SPI
highlights.usage: Board bring-up, sensor links, flash programming, and debug channels during hardware validation and factory test.

highlights.name: BLE
highlights.usage: Short-range links for service apps, cabin/operator interfaces, and master–slave modules on mobile equipment.

highlights.name: Wi‑Fi
highlights.usage: Field updates, telemetry, and connected diagnostics when machines need IP connectivity beyond the local CAN bus.

highlights.name: HIL
highlights.usage: Hardware-in-the-loop benches that validate ECU behavior before vehicle or plant integration — catching faults off the critical path.

highlights.name: V-model
highlights.usage: Requirements-to-test traceability for regulated or safety-minded embedded programs across design and verification.

highlights.name: Bring-up
highlights.usage: First power-on of new boards — clocks, rails, peripherals, and boot paths until the prototype talks on the bus.

highlights.name: J-Link
highlights.usage: Flash, breakpoint debug, and RTT-style visibility during firmware development and field failure analysis.

highlights.name: Kvaser / Peak
highlights.usage: CAN capture and injection for bus analysis, manufacturing activation sequences, and multi-ECU system debug.

highlights.name: Scope
highlights.usage: Signal integrity and timing checks on clocks, SPI, UART, and CAN edges when the software alone cannot explain the fault.

highlights.name: Git
highlights.usage: Branching, reviews, and release tagging across firmware families shared by multi-site engineering teams.
